title: T-023 — GS1-first identifier helpers (GTIN/SSCC/GLN/GRAI/GDTI parsers + check-digit)
prompt:
# T-023 — GS1-first identifier helpers (GTIN/SSCC/GLN/GRAI/GDTI parsers + check-digit)

PRD: `00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5, §10
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`

## Goal
Implement GS1 General Specs 24.0 parsers + check-digit validators for GTIN-13/14, SSCC-18, GLN-13, GRAI, GDTI. Used as preferred IDs over local SKUs.

## Implementation contract
Create packages/gs1/src/check-digit.ts exporting computeMod10(digits: string): string (GS1 standard mod-10). Create packages/gs1/src/parse.ts exporting parseGTIN(s), parseSSCC(s), parseGLN(s), parseGRAI(s), parseGDTI(s) — each returns {valid: boolean, digits: string, error?: string}. Vitest covers a known-good set: GTIN '5901234123457', SSCC '376104250021234566', plus malformed cases (length wrong, check-digit wrong).

## Files (scope)
- packages/gs1/src/check-digit.ts [create]
- packages/gs1/src/parse.ts [create]
- packages/gs1/src/__tests__/parse.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given known-good GTIN '5901234123457', when parseGTIN runs, then valid=true and digits='5901234123457'
2. Given a tampered GTIN '5901234123458' (last digit wrong), when parseGTIN runs, then valid=false and error='check_digit_mismatch'
3. Given an SSCC '376104250021234566', when parseSSCC runs, then valid=true; given a 17-digit SSCC, valid=false and error='length'

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/gs1/src/__tests__/parse.test.ts asserting check-digit and length cases before parse.ts exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/gs1 test'

## Risk red lines
- Do not invent custom mod-10 — match GS1 General Specs 24.0

## Dependencies (must be complete first)
- T-001

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED test before implementation) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.
